question_text: Which one of the following sentence sequences creates a coherent narrative? (i) Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away. (ii) She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs. (iii) Mina has bought vegetables and rice at the market, so her bags are heavy. (iv) He was leaning against the parapet, watching the traffic below. (a) (i), (i), (iv), (ii) (b) (ii), (iii), (i), (iv) (c) (iν), (ii), (i), (iii (d) (iii), (ii), (i), (iv)
solution:
To determine which sequence of sentence sequences creates a coherent narrative, we need to analyze the logical flow of the sentences. Let's examine each option step by step:

1. **Option (a): (i), (i), (iv), (ii)**
   - (i) Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
   - (i) She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
   - (iv) He was leaning against the parapet, watching the traffic below.
   - (ii) She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
   
   This sequence is not coherent because the second (i) is a repetition of the first (i), and the fourth sentence (iv) does not logically follow the third sentence (i).

2. **Option (b): (ii), (iii), (i), (iv)**
   - (ii) She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
   - (iii) Mina has bought vegetables and rice at the market, so her bags are heavy.
   - (i) Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
   - (iv) He was leaning against the parapet, watching the traffic below.
   
   This sequence is not coherent because the second sentence (iii) does not logically follow the first sentence (ii), and the third sentence (i) does not logically follow the second sentence (iii).

3. **Option (c): (i), (ii), (i), (iii)**
   - (i) Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
   - (ii) She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
   - (i) Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
   - (iii) Mina has bought vegetables and rice at the market, so her bags are heavy.
   
   This sequence is not coherent because the third sentence (i) is a repetition of the first sentence (i), and the fourth sentence (iii) does not logically follow the second sentence (ii).

4. **Option (d): (iii), (ii), (i), (iv)**
   - (iii) Mina has bought vegetables and rice at the market, so her bags are heavy.
   - (ii) She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
   - (i) Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
   - (iv) He was leaning against the parapet, watching the traffic below.
   
   This sequence is coherent because:
   - The first sentence (iii) describes Mina's activities at the market.
   - The second sentence (ii) describes her behavior after climbing the stairs.
   - The third sentence (i) describes her observation of the man on the terrace.
   - The fourth sentence (iv) describes his position relative to the parapet.

Therefore, the correct answer is \boxed{D}.
